data.title: Celestica Accelerates AI-Scale Networking: DS6000-Series 1.6TbE Switches Now Available to Order
data.description: On April 29, 2026, Celestica Inc. revealed that its DS6000-series 1.6TbE switches, aimed at data center infrastructure and advanced technology solutions, are now open for ordering by early clients. This move marks a significant shift for the platform from the development phase to being ready for orders. These switches play a crucial role in supporting the upcoming wave of generative AI and machine learning infrastructure. Celestica Inc., listed on NYSE as CLS and TSX as CLS, is a prominent player in this industry.

data.title: Brinker Raises Outlook As Chili's Extends Blazing 20-Quarter Streak
data.description: Brinker International Inc. (NYSE: EAT) released their third-quarter earnings report, surpassing Wall Street expectations with adjusted earnings of $2.90 per share, higher than the anticipated $2.87. Revenue for the quarter stood at $1.470 billion, closely aligning with the expected $1.474 billion. Notable sales figures include a 3.3% rise in comparable restaurant sales, driven by a 4.0% increase at Chili's, offset by a 4.6% decline at Maggiano's. Operating income saw an increase to $166.6 million from $156.9 million year-over-year, as operating margin expanded to 11.3% from 11.0%. Adjusted restaurant operating margin dipped to 18.4% from 18.9%. Adjusted EBITDA also saw growth, reaching $223.7 million, up from $220.6 million in the preceding year.

data.title: Emergent BioSolutions Secures Long-Term Strategic Manufacturing Contract with SAB Biotherapeutics to Advance Type 1 Diabetes Candidate, SAB-142
data.description: Today in Gaithersburg, Maryland, Emergent BioSolutions Inc. revealed a new partnership with SAB Biotherapeutics, Inc. The agreement, which focuses on aiding the production of SAB BIO’s leading program SAB-142 for autoimmune type 1 diabetes, is set to last for multiple years. The deal totals around $50 million, with $36 million tied to potential milestones and regulatory greenlights in the future.

data.title: CN Declares Second-Quarter 2026 Dividend
data.description: Montreal, April 29, 2026 - CN revealed its decision to issue a dividend for the second quarter of 2026 on their common shares. The Board of Directors gave the green light to a payment of C$0.9150 per common share. Shareholders of record as of June 9, 2026, will receive this quarterly dividend on June 30, 2026. The dividend payment reflects the company's commitment to rewarding its shareholders and maintaining financial stability.

data.title: GM To Roll Out Google Gemini AI Across 4 Million Cars: Here's What Owners Need To Know
data.description: General Motors Co. announced the integration of Alphabet Inc.'s artificial intelligence model Google Gemini into more than 4 million vehicles in the U.S. through an upcoming update. The rollout will include GMC, Cadillac, Buick, and Chevrolet vehicles from the 2022 model year onwards, featuring Google Built‑in technology. According to GM, Gemini allows for natural voice commands without the need to remember specific phrases. The AI model will provide functions such as summarizing and reading incoming texts. This move signifies a significant advancement in AI technology adoption within the automotive industry.

data.title: Bitcoin Holds $77,000, Ethereum, XRP Rebound While Dogecoin Rallies On FOMC Day
data.description: Bitcoin surpassed the $77,000 mark on Wednesday morning driven by positive sentiment following statements made by the SEC chair during the Bitcoin 2026 event. On Tuesday, Bitcoin ETFs experienced net outflows of $89.7 million, whereas Ethereum ETFs recorded net outflows of $21.8 million. Current cryptocurrency prices include Bitcoin at $77,620.71, Ethereum at $2,334.55, Solana at $85.32, XRP at $1.39, Dogecoin at $0.1100, and Shiba Inu at $0.056473. The overall market capitalization of meme coins saw a 2.8% decrease. For more details, visit a financial news website.

data.title: Snap-on to Present at Oppenheimer Industrial Growth Conference
data.description: A leading company in Kenosha, Wisconsin, Snap-on, will be participating in the Oppenheimer Industrial Growth Conference on May 6, 2026. The presentation is set to commence at 1:30 p.m. Eastern Time.

data.title: PHINIA to Participate in Bank of America’s 33rd Annual Industrials, Transportation and Airlines Key Leaders Conference
data.description: PHINIA Inc. (NYSE: PHIN), a top player in premium fuel systems, electrical systems, and aftermarket solutions, revealed it will take part in Bank of America’s 33rd Annual Industrials, Transportation, and Airlines Key Leaders Conference on May 13, 2026. The participation in the conference, set to be held in Auburn Hills, Mich., will involve in-person attendance. It will feature a Q&A segment aimed at delving into the dynamics of the markets PHINIA caters to globally and discussing the company's operations. This move comes as PHINIA continues to establish its presence as a prominent player, offering innovative solutions in its respective sectors.

data.title: XCharge North America’s GridLink Earns 2026 E+E Leader Award for Innovation in Sustainability
data.description: XCharge North America (XCharge NA), a subsidiary of XCHG Limited (Nasdaq: XCH), has been recognized for its flagship product, GridLink, in the 2026 Environment+Energy Leader Awards. GridLink is designed to enhance the North American electrical grid through high-power EV charging and battery-integrated solutions. The award highlights companies making strides in energy management and environmental performance. This recognition underscores XCharge NA's commitment to innovation and sustainability in the energy sector.
